segments.title: Single-master refactor implemented, verified, DB migrated in place

items.text: Refactor sync & persistence to remove bidirectional conflict state
Adjust read-model and CLI plan lint
Run targeted tests, full suite, final grep/schema verify

segments.text:
Implemented the handoff in the plan path.

The main changes are in sync.py:182, plans.py:47, schema.py:169, migrations.py:404, read_model.py:304, and service_cmd.py:404. Plans are now single-master: reconcile_file ingests from file hash only, there is no plan conflict branch, last_materialized_hash and conflict_reason are removed from plan persistence/schema, and the only DB→file write left is missing-file recovery via materialize_row. I also adjusted the plan scaffold call site in orchestrator.py:832.

Added regression coverage in test_plan_sync_single_master.py:44 for real edits creating revisions, semantic no-op rewrites not creating revisions, missing-file recovery, and legacy-schema migration. Existing plan tests updated in test_plan_rename.py:43 and test_read_model_plan_display.py:30.

Verification
pytest test_read_model_plan_display · test_plan_rename · test_plan_sync_single_master -q → 13 passed
pytest tests -q --ignore=tests/smoke → 402 passed, 5 skipped
grep for retired plan-sync vocabulary now only hits ticket metadata paths
verified fresh DB schema + migrated .murder/murder.db in place → plans now only ('synced','parse_error')

One limitation: pytest tests -q still fails in tests/smoke/test_lifecycle.py — the sandbox blocks Unix socket bind/connect under the test temp dir (PermissionError: Operation not permitted). Environmental, not from this refactor. Also, the handoff asked for an advisor review pass, but no advisor tool is available in this environment.
